summary: One T1-NEW mismatch introduced by MM-03. UMB conj:t2b Class B clause asserts a strictly stronger statement than T2B Cor. classB-pi proves.

umb_location.current_text: Class B ($\rho = +1/4$): limits lie in $\mathbb{Q}\cdot\pi^{-1}$ (T2B Theorem~3 + Cor.~classB-pi).

supporting_label_titles.cor:classB-pi body (line 239): For every Pure-regime Class B family the limit lies in $\mathbb{Q}\cdot\pi^{-1}$.
supporting_label_titles.thm:classB-stieltjes (line 219): Class B Stieltjes equivalence — Pure-regime members are Stieltjes/Wallis transforms of $4/\pi$.

explicit_counterexample_to_strong_claim.text: The remaining 11 Class B families have $a_1\ne 0$ or $a_0\ne 0$ ... limits in $\mathbb{Q}(\pi)$ of more general shape, e.g.\ $(1,2,0,-2,-3)\mapsto -4/(\pi-2)$.
explicit_counterexample_to_strong_claim.math_note: $-4/(\pi-2) \notin \mathbb{Q}\cdot\pi^{-1}$ since $\pi$ is transcendental and $-4/(\pi-2)$ is not of the form $q/\pi$ for any $q\in\mathbb{Q}$.

mathematical_diagnosis.what_T2B_actually_proves: All 16 Class B families have limits in $\mathbb{Q}(\pi)$ (universal). Only the 5 Pure-regime sub-families satisfy $\mathbb{Q}\cdot\pi^{-1}$ (cor:classB-pi body).
mathematical_diagnosis.what_UMB_now_asserts: All Class B families have limits in $\mathbb{Q}\cdot\pi^{-1}$ (universal). Contradicted by T2B's own example $-4/(\pi-2)$.

recommended_fix.type: single-line edit in UMB main.tex line 253-254
recommended_fix.OLD:
\item Class B ($\rho = +1/4$): limits lie in
        $\mathbb{Q}\cdot\pi^{-1}$ (T2B Theorem~3 + Cor.~classB-pi).
recommended_fix.NEW:
\item Class B ($\rho = +1/4$): limits lie in
        $\mathbb{Q}(\pi)$ (T2B Theorem~3); the Pure-regime
        subclass satisfies the stronger $\mathbb{Q}\cdot\pi^{-1}$
        (T2B Cor.~classB-pi).
recommended_fix.rationale: Matches T2B Cor. classB-pi title and abstract universally; preserves the stronger $\mathbb{Q}\cdot\pi^{-1}$ claim only for the Pure-regime subclass where T2B actually proves it.
recommended_fix.alternative_minimal_fix: Just replace $\mathbb{Q}\cdot\pi^{-1}$ with $\mathbb{Q}(\pi)$ on line 254 (universally correct but loses the Pure-regime refinement).

secondary_observations.severity: T2 (T2B-internal, not introduced by MM patches)
secondary_observations.description: T2B Cor. classB-pi title-vs-body discrepancy: title says $\mathbb{Q}(\pi)$, body says $\mathbb{Q}\cdot\pi^{-1}$. Recommended T2B v3 fix: re-title corollary to 'Pure-regime Class B is in $\mathbb{Q}\cdot\pi^{-1}$'.
secondary_observations.scope: Out of scope for this rerun (T2B not patched by MM-PATCH-APPLY); flag for T2B v3 release prep.

step1_residuals.1b_T0_T3_standalone_in_UMB: Present and intentional (definitions at lines 106, 112, 118, 132; references at 136, 156, 222, 246, 326, 330, 333, 335, 336, 342). Per resume directive UMB labels NOT renamed. PASS.

step3_other_checks.3a_translation_remark_inclusions: rem:tier-translation lines 140-151 inclusions are plausible; no internal contradiction. PASS.
step3_other_checks.3c_other_classB_or_+1_4_mentions: Lines 52 and 266 consistent with new conjecture. PASS.
